name: Brute Force
description: Adversaries may use brute force techniques to gain access to accounts when passwords are unknown or when password hashes are obtained.
detection_guidance: Monitor authentication logs for repeated failed login attempts. Alert on high volumes of authentication failures from single sources.
mitigation: Enable account lockout policies, use multi-factor authentication, implement rate limiting on authentication endpoints.

name: Brute Force: Password Guessing
description: Adversaries with no prior knowledge of legitimate credentials may try to guess passwords to gain access to accounts.
detection_guidance: Monitor for failed authentication attempts. Correlate failures by source IP and target account.
mitigation: Enforce strong password policies, implement account lockout, use fail2ban or similar tooling.

name: Brute Force: Password Spraying
description: Adversaries use a single or small list of commonly used passwords against many accounts to avoid account lockouts.
detection_guidance: Detect authentication attempts hitting many different accounts from a single source in a short time window.
mitigation: Use MFA, monitor for cross-account authentication failures from same source.

name: Valid Accounts
description: Adversaries may obtain and abuse credentials of existing accounts as a means of gaining Initial Access, Persistence, or Privilege Escalation.
detection_guidance: Monitor for unusual authentication patterns such as logins from new locations, off-hours access, or authentication after previous failures.
mitigation: Implement least privilege, use MFA, monitor for anomalous account usage.

name: Valid Accounts: Local Accounts
description: Adversaries may obtain and abuse credentials of a local account as a means of gaining access to a system.
detection_guidance: Monitor local account authentication events, especially root logins via SSH.
mitigation: Disable root SSH login (PermitRootLogin no), enforce use of sudo.

name: Abuse Elevation Control Mechanism: Sudo and Sudo Caching
description: Adversaries may use sudo to gain elevated privileges on a system. Sudo allows users to execute programs with the security privileges of another user.
detection_guidance: Monitor sudoers file changes, alert on sudo shell escapes (bash, sh, su via sudo).
mitigation: Configure sudoers with explicit command allowlists, avoid NOPASSWD, audit sudo usage.

name: Create Account
description: Adversaries may create an account to maintain access to victim systems.
detection_guidance: Monitor for new account creation in auth logs, unusual first-time logins.
mitigation: Monitor account creation events, review new accounts immediately.

name: Account Access Removal
description: Adversaries may interrupt availability of system and network resources by inhibiting access to accounts utilized by legitimate users.
detection_guidance: Monitor for account lockouts, deletion events.
mitigation: Backup account data, monitor account modifications.

name: Non-Standard Port
description: Adversaries may communicate using a protocol and port pairing that are typically not associated.
detection_guidance: Analyze network traffic for unexpected port usage in authentication logs.
mitigation: Network segmentation, firewall rules restricting unusual port combinations.

name: Remote Services
description: Adversaries may use valid accounts to log into a service specifically designed to accept remote connections.
detection_guidance: Monitor remote service access logs for unusual patterns.
mitigation: Limit remote service access, require MFA for all remote connections.
